message:
refactor(tui): redesign metrics panel for ensemble observability

Major redesign of the TUI metrics panel to display ensemble-specific
observability data instead of agent token metrics:

Dashboard panels/metrics.go:
- Replace AgentMetric struct with ensemble-focused MetricsData containing
  Coverage, Redundancy, Velocity, and Conflicts reports
- Add coverage mini-bar visualization showing category completion
- Add redundancy warning when overlap exceeds threshold
- Add velocity display showing findings per 1k tokens
- Add conflict density summary

Dashboard and commands:
- Update dashboard to integrate new metrics data flow
- Simplify command handling for metrics panel interactions
- Update tests for new panel structure

CLI additions:
- root.go: Add new CLI commands/flags
- safety.go: Enhanced safety checks

tmux client:
- Minor client improvements

E2E tests:
- ensemble_metrics_test.go: E2E tests for metrics panel
- robot_ensemble_test.go: Robot mode ensemble tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1nna","title":"Task: Create built-in webhook formatters for Slack, Discord, Teams","description":"## Overview
Implement message formatters that transform NTM events into platform-specific webhook payloads.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/webhook/formatters/:
   - Formatter interface: Format(event Event) ([]byte, error)
   - slack.go: Slack Block Kit formatting
   - discord.go: Discord embed formatting
   - teams.go: Microsoft Teams Adaptive Card formatting
   - json.go: Generic JSON (passthrough)

2. Slack formatter:
   - Use Block Kit for rich formatting
   - Color-code by event type (green=success, red=error)
   - Include actionable buttons where relevant
   - Format example:
     {
       "blocks": [
         {"type": "header", "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": "Agent Completed"}},
         {"type": "section", "text": {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": "*myproject__cc_1* finished task"}}
       ],
       "attachments": [{"color": "good", ...}]
     }

3. Discord formatter:
   - Use embed objects for rich display
   - Color, title, description, fields, timestamp
   - Thumbnail for agent type icon

4. Teams formatter:
   - Adaptive Card format
   - Mention support for @user notifications
   - Action buttons for quick responses

## Implementation Notes
- Templates for each message type
- Variable substitution: {{.AgentName}}, {{.SessionID}}, etc.
- Escape special characters per platform

## Acceptance Criteria
- All formatters produce valid platform-specific payloads
- Messages look good in each platform
- Variables substitute correctly
- Unit tests for each formatter","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:31:38.327678526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:15.757668899Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nna","depends_on_id":"bd-2o8r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nna","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nov","title":"Task: Add recovery config options","description":"## Purpose
Add configuration options for controlling session recovery behavior.

## Config Section
[recovery]
enabled = true                    # Master toggle
include_agent_mail = true         # Include recent messages
include_cm_memories = true        # Include procedural memories
include_beads_context = true      # Include BV task status
max_recovery_tokens = 2000        # Cap recovery context size
auto_inject_on_spawn = true       # Send automatically
stale_threshold_hours = 24        # Ignore context older than this

## Scope
- Add RecoveryConfig struct to internal/config/
- Parse from config.toml and environment variables
- Document options in example config
- Support per-session overrides via flags

## Acceptance Criteria
- All options configurable via config file
- Environment variable overrides work
- --no-recovery flag disables for single spawn
- Defaults are sensible (all enabled)

## Technical Notes
Follow existing config patterns in internal/config/config.go","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:35:26.451812680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:20:48.916259293Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:20:48.916209829Z","close_reason":"Added SessionRecoveryConfig to internal/config/config.go with TOML parsing and env overrides.  {"id":"bd-1nqde","title":"Task: Parity Gate CI (kernel coverage + CLI/REST parity)","description":"## Scope\nAdd CI tests that prevent drift between CLI, robot mode, and REST/OpenAPI.\n\n## Implementation\n- Validate every kernel command has CLI + REST bindings.\n- Ensure every CLI command is registered in kernel.\n- Compare generated OpenAPI vs checked‑in spec (fail on diff).\n- CLI vs REST parity tests for representative commands.\n\n## Logging\n- Log missing bindings/commands with exact IDs and paths.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: kernel coverage tests.\n- Integration: parity harness comparing normalized CLI JSON vs REST JSON.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- CI fails on missing bindings or OpenAPI drift.\n- Parity tests catch output mismatches deterministically.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:37.823284186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:24:55.757982074Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:24:55.757424424Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nqde","depends_on_id":"bd-204ic","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nqde","depends_on_id":"bd-vmwdj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ny8s","title":"Task: Write E2E tests for ensemble Robot API","description":"# Task: Robot API E2E Tests

End-to-end tests for ensemble robot commands with full JSON validation.

## Coverage
- --robot-ensemble
- --robot-ensemble-spawn
- --robot-ensemble-synthesize
- --robot-ensemble-modes (default core)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --tier=all (includes advanced)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --category=Formal (filter)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --limit/--offset (pagination)
- --robot-ensemble-presets (list)
- **advanced gating**: spawn with advanced mode without `--allow-advanced` (expect error), then with `--allow-advanced` (expect success)

## Logging Requirements
- Log raw JSON output
- Log parsed response structs
- Log session name + agent counts
- Log tier counts (core vs advanced)
- Log error_code + hint for gating failures

 {"id":"bd-1o1q1","title":"RCH: Add --robot-rch-status and --robot-rch-workers commands","description":"Add robot mode commands for RCH status and worker information.

## Command: --robot-rch-status

```bash
ntm --robot-rch-status
```

### Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "rch": {
    "enabled": true,
    "available": true,
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "workers": {
      "total": 3,
      "healthy": 2,
      "busy": 1
    },
    "session_stats": {
      "builds_total": 45,
      "builds_remote": 30,
      "builds_local": 15,
      "time_saved_seconds": 1200
    }
  }
}
```

## Command: --robot-rch-workers

```bash
ntm --robot-rch-workers [--worker=NAME]
```

### Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "workers": [
    {
      "name": "builder-1",
      "host": "192.168.1.100",
      "status": "healthy",
      "current_build": null,
      "builds_completed": 20,
      "cpu_percent": 15
    },
    {
      "name": "builder-2",
      "host": "192.168.1.101",
      "status": "busy",
      "current_build": "cargo build --release",
      "builds_completed": 25,
      "cpu_percent": 95
    }
  ]
}
```

## Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "rch not found in PATH",
  "hint": "Install rch: cargo install rch"
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/robot/rch_status.go
- internal/robot/rch_workers.go

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--robot-rch-status` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--robot-rch-workers` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--worker=NAME` filter works for specific worker
- [ ] JSON output matches schema exactly
- [ ] Missing rch returns actionable error with install hint
- [ ] Workers array includes all configured workers
- [ ] session_stats aggregates from current session
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on partial data, 2 on error

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (internal/robot/rch_status_test.go)
- [ ] Flag parsing for all options
- [ ] rch status parsing
- [ ] rch workers parsing
- [ ] JSON output serialization
- [ ] Error handling for missing rch
- [ ] Error handling for unreachable workers

### E2E Tests (scripts/e2e_robot_rch.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-RCH] Robot RCH Status Tests
set -euo pipefail
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RCH]"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX Starting robot RCH E2E tests"

# Check if rch is available
if \! command -v rch &>/dev/null; then
    echo "$LOG_PREFIX SKIP: rch not installed"
    exit 0
fi

# Test 1: RCH status
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 1: RCH status"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rch-status)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".rch.version") \!= "" ]] || exit 1

# Test 2: RCH workers
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 2: RCH workers"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rch-workers)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq ".workers | type") == "\"array\"" ]] || exit 1

# Test 3: Missing rch fallback (if applicable)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 3: Error handling"
# Mock test - verify error schema
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Error schema verified"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX All robot RCH E2E tests passed"
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:54.549811488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T17:53:32.790300Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o1q1","depends_on_id":"bd-1c9ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o1q1","depends_on_id":"bd-1ghcp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o1q1","depends_on_id":"bd-3u84u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1o2r","title":"Fix double-width character handling (emoji, CJK)","description":"## Problem
Double-width characters (emoji, CJK, some Unicode) cause column misalignment because they take 2 terminal cells but count as 1 rune.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Good News: Already Handled Correctly

**The codebase uses `lipgloss.Width()` which internally uses `go-runewidth`:**

1. **table.go:205-210** - `runeWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// runeWidth returns the visual display width of a string.
// Uses lipgloss.Width() which properly handles ANSI escape codes and
// double-width characters (CJK, emoji) that occupy 2 terminal columns.
func runeWidth(s string) int {
    return lipgloss.Width(s)
}
```

2. **layout.go:93-128** - `TruncateWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// TruncateWidth trims a string to fit within maxWidth terminal columns,
// ...Unlike TruncateRunes, this uses lipgloss.Width()
// to properly account for double-width characters (CJK, emoji) and ANSI codes.
func TruncateWidth(s string, maxWidth int, suffix string) string {
    currentWidth := lipgloss.Width(s)
    ...
}
```

3. **badges.go:687-712** - truncation uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// Uses lipgloss.Width() to properly handle ANSI codes and double-width runes.
```

### Non-Width Uses Are Correct

Found `utf8.RuneCountInString()` used in:
- **activity.go:81-82** - Counting characters for velocity metrics (chars/sec)
- **palette/model.go:1279** - Displaying "X chars" badge

Both are for CHARACTER COUNTING, not display width. Using `RuneCountInString` is correct there.

## Verification Tests Required

Add tests to verify double-width handling:

```go
func TestDoubleWidthCharacterWidth(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        input    string
        wantWidth int
    }{
        {"ASCII", "hello", 5},
        {"Emoji single", "😀", 2},
        {"Emoji sequence", "👨‍👩‍👧‍👦", 2},  // Family emoji (ZWJ sequence)
        {"CJK", "中文", 4},
        {"Mixed", "hi中文!", 7},
        {"Checkmark", "✓", 1},
        {"Warning", "⚠", 1},
        {"Full block", "█", 1},
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            got := lipgloss.Width(tc.input)
            if got != tc.wantWidth {
                t.Errorf("Width(%q) = %d, want %d", tc.input, got, tc.wantWidth)
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestTableAlignmentWithEmoji(t *testing.T) {
    table := NewStyledTable("Status", "Name", "Count")
    table.AddRow("✓", "Test 1", "10")
    table.AddRow("⚠", "Test 2 中文", "20")
    table.AddRow("✗", "Test 😀 3", "30")
    
    rendered := table.Render()
    lines := strings.Split(rendered, "\n")
    
    // All lines should have same width (accounting for borders)
    var widths []int
    for _, line := range lines {
        if strings.TrimSpace(line) != "" {
            widths = append(widths, lipgloss.Width(line))
        }
    }
    
    for i := 1; i < len(widths); i++ {
        if widths[i] != widths[0] {
            t.Errorf("line %d width %d != first line width %d", i, widths[i], widths[0])
        }
    }
}

func TestTruncateWidthWithEmoji(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        input    string
        maxWidth int
        want     string
    }{
        {"hello😀world", 8, "hello😀…"},  // emoji is 2 wide
        {"中文测试", 5, "中文…"},          // CJK truncation
        {"test中", 5, "test…"},           // Mixed truncation
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        got := layout.TruncateWidth(tc.input, tc.maxWidth, "…")
        gotWidth := lipgloss.Width(got)
        if gotWidth > tc.maxWidth {
            t.Errorf("TruncateWidth(%q, %d) = %q (width %d), exceeds max", 
                tc.input, tc.maxWidth, got, gotWidth)
        }
    }
}
```

## E2E Visual Test (VHS)
```tape
# double_width_test.tape
Output double_width_test.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200

Type "ntm --demo-mode"
Sleep 2s

# The demo should show agents with emoji icons
# Verify alignment visually
Screenshot double_width_check.png
```

## Status: Verification Only

The implementation is already correct. This bead becomes a verification/documentation task:
- [x] runeWidth() uses lipgloss.Width()
- [x] TruncateWidth() uses lipgloss.Width() 
- [x] Non-width uses are appropriate (char counting)
- [ ] Add unit tests for double-width scenarios
- [ ] Verify in VHS recording

## Priority Adjustment  
Changed to P3 - verification task. Implementation already correct.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:10:46.114845445Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:09:25.920269264Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:09:25.920199492Z","close_reason":"Verification complete: runeWidth() and TruncateWidth() use lipgloss.Width(). Added comprehensive unit tests for double-width scenarios including emoji (🎉, 😀, 🤖), CJK (日本語, 中文), and mixed content. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o2r","depends_on_id":"bd-1ay2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o2r","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3s2f","title":"EPIC: Webhook System for External Integrations","description":"## Overview
Implement an event-driven webhook system that triggers HTTP callbacks on NTM events, enabling integration with Slack, Discord, PagerDuty, CI/CD systems, and custom automation.

## Problem Statement
NTM operates as a closed system. Users can't:
- Get notified in Slack when agents complete work
- Trigger CI pipelines when all agents finish
- Alert PagerDuty when errors occur
- Build custom dashboards from live data
- Integrate with existing DevOps tooling

## Key Features
1. Configurable webhook endpoints per event type
2. Built-in formatters for Slack, Discord, Teams
3. Retry logic with exponential backoff
4. Event filtering by session, agent type, severity
5. Payload templates with variable substitution

## Event Types
- agent.started: Agent session created
- agent.completed: Agent finished task
- agent.error: Agent encountered error
- agent.idle: Agent became idle
- session.created: New NTM session
- session.ended: Session terminated
- bead.assigned: Bead assigned to agent
- bead.completed: Bead marked done

## Configuration (ntm.yaml)
webhooks:
  - url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/xxx
    events: [agent.completed, agent.error]
    formatter: slack
    filter:
      session: myproject*
      severity: [warning, error, critical]
  - url: https://my-api.com/ntm-events
    events: [*]
    formatter: json

## Success Criteria
- Webhooks fire reliably on configured events
- Built-in formatters produce valid Slack/Discord messages
- Failures don't block NTM operation
- Easy to configure without code changes

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/webhook/manager.go (new - webhook orchestration)
- internal/webhook/formatters/ (new - Slack, Discord, JSON formatters)
- internal/config/webhooks.go (new - webhook config parsing)
- internal/events/emitter.go (event emission points)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3s2wn","title":"Task: tmux pipe-pane streaming integration","description":"## Scope\nStream live pane output using tmux pipe-pane with fallback capture-pane polling.\n\n## Implementation\n- On pane spawn: tmux pipe-pane -o to internal streamer.\n- Streamer emits WS events + writes to log for replay.\n- Fallback to capture-pane polling if pipe-pane fails.\n\n## Logging\n- Log pipe-pane attach/detach, fallback activation, pane IDs.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: streamer handles chunk boundaries and encoding.\n- Integration: spawn session, verify WS output matches pane content.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Live output latency < 100ms under normal load.\n- Fallback works when pipe-pane unavailable.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"claude-opus-4.5","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:51:44.010812813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:31:28.933982268Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:31:28.933849621Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3s2wn","depends_on_id":"bd-204ic","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3s2wn","depends_on_id":"bd-yn7qe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3sha","title":"Task: Dashboard help respects minimal/full verbosity","description":"## Overview
Replace tier‑based dashboard hints with the **help verbosity** model (minimal/full) and optional debug mode, aligning with CLI help simplification.

## Deliverables
1. Update internal/tui/dashboard/help.go:
   - Minimal: essential keys only (navigate/select/quit)
   - Full: all standard keys (refresh/zoom/etc.)
   - Debug: add debug keys/panels when `--debug` or `NTM_DEBUG=1`

2. Status bar hint:
   - Show current help verbosity: `Help: minimal` or `Help: full`

3. Panel visibility:
   - Minimal: core panels (activity/status)
   - Full: all standard panels
   - Debug: include internal panels if available

## Implementation Notes
- Read verbosity from config at dashboard init
- Cache to avoid repeated file reads
- Update hints when verbosity changes (rare)

## Tests
- Unit: mapping of verbosity → key list
- E2E: dashboard rendering in minimal/full/debug with logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dashboard respects help verbosity setting
- Keybinding hints filter appropriately
- Verbosity indicator shows in status bar
- E2E tests for minimal/full/debug rendering","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:38.988102962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:51:55.266727384Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3sha","depends_on_id":"bd-352n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3sha","depends_on_id":"bd-vcmnv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3sl6a","title":"Task: Write E2E test for observability metrics","description":"E2E tests for coverage map, redundancy score, velocity metrics, and conflict density.

## Test Harness
- Use deterministic fixtures (no real model calls)
- Feed synthetic ModeOutput + token counts through metrics pipeline
- Run via existing e2e suite in `e2e/`

## Test Cases

### Coverage Map
```go
// e2e/ensemble_metrics_test.go
func TestE2E_Metrics_CoverageMap(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: fixed mode assignments with known categories
    // Verify: Coverage map shows correct category coverage
    // Verify: Blind spots correctly identified
}
```

### Redundancy Score
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_RedundancyScore_LowRedundancy(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: outputs with disjoint findings
    // Verify: Low redundancy score
}

func TestE2E_Metrics_RedundancyScore_HighRedundancy(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: overlapping findings + evidence pointers
    // Verify: High redundancy score + suggestions
}
```

### Findings Velocity
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_FindingsVelocity(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: outputs with known findings + token counts
    // Verify: Velocity per mode + overall
    // Verify: Underperformers flagged
}
```

### Conflict Density
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_ConflictDensity(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: conflict list from AuditReport (preferred) + fallback heuristic
    // Verify: Conflicts detected and counted
    // Verify: High-conflict pairs identified
}
```

### Post-Run Report
```go
func TestE2E_Metrics_PostRunReport(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: complete ensemble run with metrics summary
    // Verify: Report includes all metrics + suggestions
}
```

## Logging
- t.Logf metric inputs (mode IDs, tokens, findings counts)
- t.Logf computed metric values
- Log threshold comparisons and selected suggestions

 {"id":"bd-3u59m","title":"Implement AutoRespawner for limit-hit recovery","description":"## AutoRespawner: Core Interface for Agent Recovery

### Purpose
Implement the AutoRespawner that handles automatic agent recovery when usage limits are hit. Integrates with LimitDetector events and AccountRotator for seamless recovery.

### IMPORTANT: Leverage Existing Code
NTM already has `internal/context/rotation.go` with:
- PaneSpawner interface for creating/killing panes
- DefaultPaneSpawner implementation
- SendKeys for sending commands to panes

AutoRespawner should USE these existing abstractions, not duplicate them.

### Implementation Details

**File:** `internal/swarm/auto_respawner.go`

**Interface:**
```go
// AutoRespawner handles automatic agent recovery.
type AutoRespawner struct {
    // Config holds respawn settings
    Config *config.SwarmConfig
    
    // LimitDetector provides limit events
    LimitDetector *LimitDetector
    
    // AccountRotator switches accounts (optional)
    AccountRotator *AccountRotator
    
    // PromptInjector re-sends marching orders
    PromptInjector *PromptInjector
    
    // PaneSpawner from context package (REUSE EXISTING)
    PaneSpawner context.PaneSpawner
    
    // eventChan emits respawn events
    eventChan chan RespawnEvent
}

// RespawnResult tracks a respawn attempt.
type RespawnResult struct {
    Success         bool          `json:"success"`
    SessionPane     string        `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType       string        `json:"agent_type"`
    AccountRotated  bool          `json:"account_rotated"`
    PreviousAccount string        `json:"previous_account,omitempty"`
    NewAccount      string        `json:"new_account,omitempty"`
    Duration        time.Duration `json:"duration"`
    Error           string        `json:"error,omitempty"`
    RespawnedAt     time.Time     `json:"respawned_at"`
}
```

**Respawn Sequence:**
```
1. Receive LimitEvent from LimitDetector
2. Log respawn start
3. Kill stuck agent:
   a. Send /exit (for codex) or Ctrl+C twice (for claude)
   b. Wait 2s for graceful exit
   c. If still running, kill process via PID
4. (Optional) Rotate account via AccountRotator
5. Clear pane: tmux send-keys -t <pane> 'clear' Enter
6. Respawn agent: Send agent command (cc/cod/gmi)
7. Wait for agent ready (configurable delay, default 5s)
8. Re-inject marching orders via PromptInjector
9. Emit RespawnEvent
10. Log respawn complete
```

**Kill Sequences by Agent Type:**
```go
func (r *AutoRespawner) killAgent(sessionPane, agentType string) error {
    switch agentType {
    case "cc":
        // Claude: Double Ctrl+C with 100ms gap (CRITICAL timing)
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x03", false)
        time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x03", false)
    case "cod":
        // Codex: /exit command
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "/exit", true)
    case "gmi":
        // Gemini: Escape then Ctrl+C
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x1b", false)
        time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
        r.PaneSpawner.SendKeys(sessionPane, "\x03", false)
    }
    return nil
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log respawn start: `[AutoRespawner] respawn_start session_pane=%s agent_type=%s`
- Log kill attempt: `[AutoRespawner] killing_agent session_pane=%s method=%s`
- Log account rotation: `[AutoRespawner] account_rotated old=%s new=%s`
- Log agent spawn: `[AutoRespawner] spawning_agent session_pane=%s`
- Log prompt injection: `[AutoRespawner] injecting_prompt session_pane=%s`
- Log respawn complete: `[AutoRespawner] respawn_complete session_pane=%s success=%t duration=%v`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Uses existing PaneSpawner interface from context package
- [ ] Agent-specific kill sequences
- [ ] Configurable timeouts and delays
- [ ] Integration with AccountRotator (optional)
- [ ] Integration with PromptInjector
- [ ] Event emission for audit trail
- [ ] Max retry limits per pane
- [ ] Detailed structured logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:13.205417120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:40:18.538092929Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:40:18.537904134Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: AutoRespawner with agent-specific kill sequences, configurable timeouts, AccountRotator integration, PromptInjector integration, event emission, max retry limits, and comprehensive logging. All tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-150ij","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-1bruk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-1v922","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3u59m","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
